title: Extract a deep 'run a translated completion' lifecycle module; shrink the three handlers to wiring

body.summary: The request lifecycle is copy-pasted across the three main route handlers. Each repeats the same dance and only the three translator functions (request / response / stream) actually vary. The streaming-detection predicate is byte-identical in all three, and error-envelope shaping is inconsistent.
body.problem: All three handlers repeat: checkRateLimit(state) -> parse payload + consola.debug -> translate request -> if (state.manualApprove) await awaitApproval() -> createChatCompletions -> if (isNonStreaming(response)) branch -> translate/stream. isNonStreaming is BYTE-IDENTICAL in all three: chat-completions/handler.ts:66-68, messages/handler.ts:89-91, responses/handler.ts:174-176 (all `(response): response is ChatCompletionResponse => Object.hasOwn(response, 'choices')`). Every route.ts wraps its handler in an identical try { return await handler(c) } catch (e) { return await forwardError(c, e) }. Error-envelope shaping is INCONSISTENT: responses/handler.ts:178-187 has a bespoke errorTypeForStatus (Anthropic-style type strings) called twice inline, while chat and messages fall back to forwardError's hardcoded type:'error' (lib/error.ts:37,48). The only genuine variation across handlers is the translators; chat is a passthrough, messages adds translateToOpenAI/translateToAnthropic/translateChunkToAnthropicEvents, responses adds translateResponsesToOpenAI/translateNonStreamingResponse/translateChunkToResponsesEvents + transport-cut finalization.
body.solution: Create a deep lifecycle module (e.g. runTranslatedCompletion) that owns rate-limit, manual-approval, createChatCompletions call, streaming detection, and error-envelope mapping -- parameterized by the per-endpoint translator functions (translateRequest, translateNonStreamingResponse, makeStreamRenderer) and an error-vocabulary selector. Handlers shrink to: parse payload + supply translators. Fold the single isNonStreaming definition and the status->type map into this module. Decide one consistent error-envelope story (per-client type vocabularies selected by a parameter, not copy-paste).
body.benefits: Locality: request-flow policy (approval gating, streaming detection, error typing) lives once instead of three times. Leverage: a new endpoint supplies three functions instead of a 90-140-line handler (responses/handler.ts is ~187 lines with an eslint-disable max-lines-per-function). Tests: the lifecycle gets one test surface; error typing becomes consistent across all clients rather than 1 bespoke + 2 generic.
body.deletion_test: Inlining the lifecycle module back into handlers reproduces the same rate-limit/approval/streaming-detection/error code 3x -- complexity reappears across callers.
body.risk_notes: MEDIUM risk: the responses streaming path has bespoke transport-cut / [DONE] finalization (handler.ts:106-171) with no analog in the other handlers. The makeStreamRenderer seam must be expressive enough to host that without forcing it back up into shared code. The chat-completions handler also has model lookup + token-count + max_tokens defaulting (handler.ts:23-48) that the others lack -- keep that as handler-local pre-translation, not lifecycle.
acceptance_criteria: Rate-limit + manual-approval gating defined exactly once
Each route.ts try/catch -> forwardError wrapper either centralized or justified
Error-envelope vocabulary selected by parameter; no bespoke inline copy in responses handler
bun test passes; bun run typecheck passes; bun run lint passes
out_of_scope: chat-completions-specific token counting / max_tokens defaulting -- stays handler-local

progress.note: Task created from improve-codebase-architecture review. Handler boilerplate + identical isNonStreaming + inconsistent error envelopes verified by reading all three handlers, server.ts, and lib/error.ts.

Confirmed current code matches spec: isNonStreaming byte-identical in all 3 handlers; gating sequence (checkRateLimit -> ... -> if(manualApprove) awaitApproval) repeated 3x; responses/handler.ts has bespoke errorTypeForStatus called twice; all 3 route.ts wrap handlers in identical try/catch->forwardError. Plan: small surgical shared seams in src/lib/completion-lifecycle.ts (NOT a mega-abstraction); leave the 3 distinct streaming loops untouched.

STEP 1 (commit 7356629): added src/lib/completion-lifecycle.ts with isNonStreaming (defined once), enterCompletion (rate-limit gate), applyRequestGating (manual-approval gate), and parameterized errorTypeForStatus(status, vocab). STEP 2 (commit pending): repointed all 3 handlers to import these; deleted the 3 local isNonStreaming copies and the bespoke errorTypeForStatus in responses/handler.ts (now calls shared one with vocab="anthropic", byte-identical strings). Streaming loops + chat-completions token/max_tokens logic left handler-local. Gates: bun test 111 pass / 0 fail (responses-stream 16/16, transport-cut preserved), typecheck exit 0, lint exit 0.

STEP 3 (commit 0feaad5): added withErrorForwarding HOF to completion-lifecycle.ts and replaced the identical try/catch->forwardError block in all 4 route definitions (chat-completions, messages '/', messages '/count_tokens', responses). STEP 4 (commit pending): added tests/completion-lifecycle.test.ts (4 tests) covering isNonStreaming true/false and errorTypeForStatus for both vocabularies. All 6 acceptance criteria met: (1) isNonStreaming defined once in completion-lifecycle.ts; (2) rate-limit + manual-approval gating defined once (enterCompletion/applyRequestGating); (3) route try/catch centralized via withErrorForwarding; (4) error vocabulary parameterized, no bespoke inline copy in responses handler; (5) responses transport-cut/[DONE] preserved (tests/responses-stream.test.ts 16/16 pass); (6) final gates: bun test 115 pass / 0 fail, typecheck exit 0, lint exit 0. Out-of-scope respected: chat-completions token-count/max_tokens defaulting and count-tokens-handler untouched. Handing off to independent verification subagent.
